heading: Fire management assistance
label: 5187 Fire management assistance
notes: Amendments2018—Subsecs. (d), (e). added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 115–2542000— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The President is authorized to provide assistance, including grants, equipment, supplies, and personnel, to any State for the suppression of any fire on publicly or privately owned forest or grassland which threatens such destruction as would constitute a major disaster.”Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President is authorized to provide assistance, including grants, equipment, supplies, and personnel, to any State or local government for the mitigation, management, and control of any fire on public or private forest land or grassland that threatens such destruction as would constitute a major disaster.

children.heading: Coordination with State and tribal departments of forestry
children.label: b Coordination with State and tribal departments of forestry
children.text: In providing assistance under this section, the President shall coordinate with State and tribal departments of forestry.

children.heading: Essential assistance
children.label: c Essential assistance
children.text: In providing assistance under this section, the President may use the authority provided under .section 5170b of this title

children.heading: Hazard mitigation assistance
children.label: d Hazard mitigation assistance
children.text: Whether or not a major disaster is declared, the President may provide hazard mitigation assistance in accordance with in any area affected by a fire for which assistance was provided under this section.section 5170c of this title

children.heading: Rules and regulations
children.label: e Rules and regulations
children.text: The President shall prescribe such rules and regulations as are necessary to carry out this section.
